data.title: Hamilton Insurance Group reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Hamilton Insurance Group press release (HG): Q4 net income of $172.2 million, or $1.69 per diluted share, and operating income of $168.2 million, or $1.65 per diluted share.
  • Gross premiums written of $669.0 million, an increase of 23.0% compared to the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Net premiums earned of $576.7 million, an increase of 19.7% compared to the fourth quarter of 2024.

data.title: Bandwidth outlines 16% revenue growth target for 2026 amid accelerating enterprise AI adoption and $80M share repurchase program
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Bandwidth Inc. (BAND) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO David Morken reported a solid fourth quarter, emphasizing "sustained business performance and strengthening fundamentals." He highlighted record $1 million-plus enterprise deals in Q4, strong progress with high-margin innovation, and tangible results from AI voice tools, the Trust portfolio, the Global Communications Cloud, and Maestro orchestration software.
  • Morken stated, "When we reported 2022 results, we set ambitious 4-year goals extending through 2026, a 15% to 20% revenue compound annual growth rate, a 5 percentage point increase in gross margin to 60%, a 20% EBITDA margin, a 15% free cash flow margin and $125 million in cumulative free cash flow." He acknowledged that messaging market dynamics will likely prevent achieving the revenue CAGR target but confirmed Bandwidth is "fully on track" for margin and free cash flow objectives, having already surpassed the $125 million cumulative free cash flow goal.
  • Bandwidth announced its first-ever share repurchase program, reflecting confidence in its "durability of our business model and our ability to generate cash while simultaneously investing in our future."
  • Morken described AI-driven enterprise wins, including a top U.S. insurance group and a top 10 U.S. bank, as well as momentum in RCS messaging and a quadrupling of third-party conversational AI developers on the platform in the last six months.
  • CFO Daryl Raiford reported, "In the fourth quarter of 2025, strong execution drove solid revenue and record levels of profitability and free cash flow. Total revenue saw a 12% year-over-year increase on an organic basis." Both Voice and Messaging segments delivered double-digit growth, with voice up 11% and messaging up 12% year-over-year.
  • Raiford noted, "EBITDA margin reached 17%, reflecting improved pricing and mix and continued progress on profitability."

Outlook

  • Raiford outlined 2026 full year guidance: "total revenue growth of approximately 16% year-over-year, including cloud communications growth of approximately 10%, adjusted EBITDA improvement of nearly 30% year-over-year, in line with our aim to achieve a 20% full year adjusted EBITDA margin and non-GAAP earnings per share of approximately $1.66 to $1.74, representing growth of approximately 19%."
  • The company expects "continued accelerating revenue growth in voice," driven by AI-influenced usage, large deals, software services, and geographic expansion.
  • Political campaign messaging is projected to contribute "roughly 2.5% of cloud communications revenue" in 2026.
  • Management confirmed that 2026 outlook is "fully on track to achieve our goals for gross margin, EBITDA margin and free cash flow margin."

Financial Results

  • Bandwidth reported total revenue of $754 million for the full year 2025, up 10% organically year-over-year, non-GAAP gross margin of 58%, adjusted EBITDA of $93 million, and free cash flow of $57 million.
  • Enterprise Voice customer category grew 21% for the year, driven by a record number of $1 million-plus deals. The enterprise cohort added in 2025 represents 15% of total enterprise revenue, with over 40% of growth from accounts added in the past three years.
  • Global Voice Plans revenue grew 8%, more than doubling compared to 2024. Programmable messaging grew 7% organically.
  • Software services revenue exited 2025 at a $15 million annualized run rate, ahead of previous expectations, and is now attached to all $1 million-plus deals.
  • Customer name retention rate remains above 99%, and organic net retention is 107%. Average annual revenue per customer ended 2025 at $232,000, up from $171,000 three years ago.

Q&A

  • Arjun Bhatia, William Blair & Company: Asked about Q4 trends in Enterprise Voice and the impact of large deals on 2026 growth. Raiford responded that annual growth was 21% and that "deployment and ramping into 2026" is expected to drive further growth. Software services are now critical to "nearly every deal."
  • Bhatia followed up on political campaign messaging, with Raiford stating "political campaign contribution this year will be roughly 2.5% of cloud communications revenue."
  • Erik Suppiger, B. Riley Securities: Questioned the difference between cloud communication growth (10%) and total revenue growth (16%). Raiford attributed the difference to "surcharge growth rate for carrier messaging surcharges," impacted by carrier price increases.
  • Suppiger also asked about competition from Twilio in voice, with Raiford responding "we're not" seeing more competition, noting recent customer wins were from other carriers.
  • Patrick Walravens, Citizens JMP Securities: Inquired about integration with Google Conversational AI and Bandwidth's competitive moat. Raiford explained Maestro's role in facilitating complex integrations and described the moat as "a mile wide, filled with oil and lit on fire," citing global infrastructure and structural margin advantages.
  • Joshua Reilly, Needham & Company: Asked about the pipeline for Voice AI, with Raiford stating "AI is a component of all enterprise conversations," and projecting 2026 as a "vital year for AI adoption to go from experimenting to real scale and deployment."
  • Reilly also asked about composition of $1 million-plus deals, with Chief Product Officer John Bell emphasizing the "move to AI is reinforcing the value proposition and showing up in these customer conversations."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a positive tone, focused on enterprise growth, AI adoption, and revenue composition, with follow-up on political messaging and competitive dynamics.
  • Management's tone was confident during prepared remarks, frequently using phrases like "we are confident" and "on track to achieve our goals," and remained detailed and assertive in Q&A, especially when describing competitive moats and AI momentum.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management showed increased focus on AI voice adoption and large enterprise deals, with management expressing even greater confidence in financial and strategic targets.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw a shift from highlighting product launches and customer events to emphasizing record enterprise deal wins, AI developer engagement, and surpassing free cash flow goals.
  • Guidance for 2026 is more specific, with explicit projections for revenue, EBITDA margin, and EPS, while acknowledging headwinds in messaging revenue CAGR.
  • Analyst questions evolved from onboarding and product adoption timelines to deeper inquiries about AI monetization and competitive positioning.
  • Management's tone grew more assertive regarding Bandwidth's moat and durability, while analysts remained focused on the enterprise AI opportunity and revenue drivers.
  • Key metrics such as customer retention, software services revenue, and enterprise deal composition were given more prominence this quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted that "market dynamics, particularly in messaging, will almost certainly keep us short of our multiyear revenue CAGR target."
  • Raiford highlighted the reliance on carrier surcharge pricing for total revenue growth differences.
  • Analysts raised questions about the impact of political campaign messaging and competitive threats from other CPaaS providers.
  • Management emphasized ongoing monitoring of political messaging contributions and continued investment in innovation to maintain competitive advantages.

Final Takeaway

Bandwidth enters 2026 with strong enterprise momentum, driven by accelerated adoption of AI-powered voice solutions, a record number of large enterprise deals, and robust financial performance. The company projects 16% revenue growth, a 20% EBITDA margin, and anticipates exceeding prior free cash flow targets, while launching its inaugural $80 million share repurchase program. Management underscores confidence in Bandwidth's business model, customer durability, and the expanding role of AI and software services as central to long-term value creation for shareholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: American States Water projects $185M–$225M in 2026 utility capex as rate base climbs 11.3% CAGR
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: American States Water Company (AWR) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Robert Sprowls, CEO, President & Director, stated that "2025 was a very productive and positive year for the company." He highlighted that Golden State Water Company received the final decision from the CPUC on its general rate case for 2025 through 2027, and the electric subsidiary received its rate decision for 2023 through 2026. Both were described as "constructive regulatory outcomes and position us to continue investing in our utility infrastructure for safe and reliable services for generations to come."
  • The CEO announced, "we delivered strong financial results for 2025 with reported earnings that were $0.20 per share higher compared to 2024 or $0.33 per share higher as adjusted."
  • Sprowls detailed expansion, noting that Golden State Water completed a transaction to own and operate water and wastewater systems for a new community expected to reach 1,300 customer connections and received approval to provide water to another community projected to serve up to 3,800 connections in five years and 17,500 dwelling units long term. Additionally, Golden State Water signed an agreement to acquire water system assets in Norwalk, serving about 900 customers.
  • The company invested $210.9 million in regulated utility infrastructure in 2025 and raised the quarterly dividend by 8.3% for its 71st consecutive year of increases.
  • Eva Tang, Senior VP of Finance, CFO, Corporate Secretary & Treasurer, reported, "Adjusted earnings for AWR consolidated increased $0.18 per share over the prior year when excluding the impact of a onetime tax benefit recorded in the fourth quarter of 2024." Tang added, "reported consolidated earnings were $0.74 per share as compared to $0.75 per share for the fourth quarter of 2024 or $0.56 per share as adjusted for that quarter."

Outlook

  • The company projects 2026 company-funded capital expenditures to reach $185 million to $225 million.
  • Robert Sprowls indicated ASUS earnings are projected at $0.63 to $0.67 per share for 2026.
  • Sprowls reiterated confidence in sustained rate base growth, stating, "Golden State Water anticipates a robust and sustained growth in its rate base over the next few years."

Financial Results

  • Consolidated earnings for the full year 2025 were $3.37 per share as reported versus $3.17 per share for 2024. Adjusted, 2025 earnings were $3.37 per share compared to $3.04 per share for 2024, representing a $0.33 per share increase.
  • Golden State Water reported $0.50 per share for the quarter, compared to $0.52 per share in 2024, or $0.39 per share as adjusted.
  • Electric segment reported $0.11 per share for the quarter, compared to $0.13 per share in 2024, or a $0.04 per share increase as adjusted.
  • ASUS earnings were $0.16 per share for the quarter, up from $0.11 per share last year.
  • Consolidated revenue for the quarter increased by $21.2 million compared to 2024, with water segment revenue growing by $17.4 million and ASUS revenue by $9.5 million.
  • Net cash provided by operating activity was $229.7 million for 2025, up from $198.7 million in 2024.
  • The company raised $67 million under its at-the-market offering program during the year.

Q&A

  • There were no analyst questions during this quarter’s call, as confirmed by the operator: "At this time, there is no one in our queue. So this concludes our question-and-answer session."

Sentiment Analysis

  • With no analyst participation, there was no expression of external sentiment. Management maintained a positive and confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing regulatory achievements, rate base growth, dividend increases, and strong financial results. Sprowls described the year as "very productive and positive" and reiterated confidence in future growth.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, where analysts raised detailed questions on ASUS contracts, rate base translation, and M&A, this quarter’s absence of analyst interaction eliminated any direct challenges or skepticism, resulting in a uniformly positive management narrative.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company continued to report robust earnings growth and capital investment, with adjusted EPS rising by $0.33 per share year-over-year.
  • Guidance for ASUS earnings in 2026 was maintained at $0.63 to $0.67 per share, consistent with prior communication.
  • Rate base growth was highlighted, with the adopted average water rate base increasing from $980.4 million in 2021 to $1.673.2 billion in 2026, a 11.3% compound annual growth rate.
  • The absence of analyst Q&A this quarter contrasted with the previous quarter’s active discussion on contract timing, rate base calculation, and strategic direction, providing fewer external challenges and a more unopposed management perspective.
  • Management’s tone remained confident and forward-looking in both quarters, but the lack of questioning in Q4 removed the critical dialogue seen previously.

Risks and Concerns

  • Sprowls cautioned that "without the continuation of a full revenue decoupling mechanism and a full cost balancing account for water supply, the company may be subject to future volatility in revenues and earnings as a result of fluctuations in water consumption by its customers and changes in water supply source mix."
  • The company noted a nearly 4% decrease in water consumption compared to amounts adopted in the general rate case but stated that the net impact from source mix and consumption fluctuations "did not have a material impact on Golden State Water's 2025 earnings."
  • Management acknowledged exposure to volatility due to regulatory changes and customer usage patterns but highlighted ongoing infrastructure investment and expansion efforts.

Final Takeaway

American States Water Company reported a strong finish to 2025, driven by favorable regulatory outcomes, robust rate base growth, and continued expansion in both regulated and contracted service businesses. The company invested heavily in infrastructure, executed strategic acquisitions, and delivered higher adjusted earnings per share, while projecting continued capital investment and earnings growth in 2026. Management emphasized confidence in the company’s outlook, highlighting both its dividend track record and its ability to navigate regulatory and operational challenges as it scales its customer and asset base.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Floor & Decor Holdings GAAP EPS of $0.36 beats by $0.01, revenue of $1.13B in-line
data.description:
• Floor & Decor Holdings press release (FND): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.36 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $1.13B (+2.7% Y/Y) in-line.
    • Adjusted EBITDA* of $119.4 million decreased 0.3% from $119.8 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024.

    For the Fiscal Year Ended December 25, 2025

    • Net sales of $4,684.1 million increased 5.1% from $4,455.8 million in fiscal 2024.
    • Comparable store sales decreased 1.8%.
    • We opened 20 new warehouse stores, closed one warehouse store, and opened a new distribution center.
    • Operating income of $270.1 million increased 5.4% from $256.2 million in fiscal 2024. Operating margin of 5.8% was flat compared to fiscal 2024.
    • Net income of $208.6 million increased 1.3% from $205.9 million in fiscal 2024. Diluted EPS of $1.92 increased 1.1% from $1.90 in fiscal 2024.
    • Adjusted EBITDA* of $538.2 million increased 5.0% from $512.5 million in fiscal 2024.

    *Non-GAAP financial measure. Please see “Non-GAAP Financial Measures” and “Reconciliation of GAAP to Non-GAAP Financial Measures” below for more information.

data.title: Gaming and Leisure Properties FFO of $1.16 beats by $0.18, revenue of $407M beats by $0.98M
data.description:
• Gaming and Leisure Properties press release (GLPI): Q4 FFO of $1.16 beats by $0.18.
  • Revenue of $407M (+4.5% Y/Y) beats by $0.98M.

  • 2026 Guidance

    Reflecting the current operating and competitive environment, the Company is providing AFFO guidance for the full year 2026 based on the following assumptions and other factors:

    • The guidance does not include the impact on operating results from any possible future acquisitions or dispositions, future capital markets activity, or other future non-recurring transactions other than: anticipated fundings of approximately $575 million to $650 million related to current development projects which will be funded relatively evenly by quarter throughout 2026; $225 million of funding for PENN’s Aurora facility late in second quarter of 2026; the completion of the Lincoln acquisition for $700 million in February of 2026; and, the anticipated settlement of $363.3 million of our forward equity on June 1, 2026.
    • The guidance assumes there will be no material changes in applicable legislation, regulatory environment, world events, including weather, recent consumer trends, economic conditions, oil prices, competitive landscape or other circumstances beyond our control that may adversely affect the Company's results of operations.

    The Company estimates AFFO for the year ending December 31, 2026 will be between $1.207 billion and $1.222 billion, or between $4.06 and $4.11 per diluted share and OP/LTIP units.

data.title: Newmont Non-GAAP EPS of $2.52 beats by $0.49, revenue of $6.81B beats by $560M
data.description:
• Newmont press release (NEM): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.52 beats by $0.49.
  • Revenue of $6.81B (+20.5% Y/Y) beats by $560M.
    • Achieved full-year production and cost guidance with production of 5.7 million attributable gold ounces from Newmont's core portfolio, for a total of 5.9 million attributable gold ounces, as well as 28 million ounces of silver and 135 thousand tonnes of copper; gold by-product AISC was $1,358 per ounce, with co-product AISC of $1,609 per ounce
    • Reported Net Income of $7.2 billion, Adjusted Net Income (ANI)2 of $7.6 billion or $6.89 per diluted share and Adjusted EBITDA2 of $13.5 billion for the year, with fourth quarter ANI2 of $2.8 billion or $2.52 per diluted share
    • Generated $10.3 billion of cash from operating activities, net of working capital impacts of $210 million; reported an all-time annual record $7.3 billion in Free Cash Flow2 for the year, including a record $2.8 billion in the fourth quarter
    • Announced an enhanced capital allocation framework3 creating a predictable pathway to per share dividend growth; declared an increased dividend of $0.26 per share of common stock for the fourth quarter of 20251
    • Returned $3.4 billion of capital to shareholders through share repurchases and dividend payments in 2025
    • To date, Newmont has executed and settled total trades of common stock repurchases of $3.6 billion since the initial program authorization in 2024; $2.4 billion remains under the previously authorized programs of $6.0 billion4
    • Reduced debt by $3.4 billion in 2025, ending the year in a net cash position of $2.1 billion2, with $7.6 billion in cash and $11.6 billion in total liquidity5
    • Completed the non-core divestiture program, generating $4.5 billion in total after-tax proceeds to date from announced transactions, inclusive of $134 million from the sale of equity shares in Greatland Resources in January 20266
    • Declared total attributable gold reserves of 118.2 million ounces and resources of 148.7 million ounces7; significant exposure to other metals, including 12.5 million tonnes of copper reserves and 442 million ounces of silver reserves
    • Declared commercial production at Ahafo North in Ghana on October 24, 2025, adding profitable gold production over an initial thirteen-year mine life
    • Announced approval for the Lihir Nearshore Barrier mine life extension, unlocking future access to production of over 5 million gold ounces and extending Lihir's mine life beyond 20408

    2026 Guidance

    • Attributable production for 2026 is expected to be approximately 5.3 million gold ounces, including over 3.9 million gold ounces from Newmont's managed operations
    • Gold by-product AISC2 is expected to be $1,680 per ounce, benefitting from profitable production of other metals
    • Sustaining capital spend of approximately $1.95 billion to advance critical tailings facility work at Cadia and Boddington, as well as other important investments to enhance the integrity and longevity of Newmont's portfolio
    • Development capital spend of approximately $1.4 billion to progress key near-term development projects including the Cadia Panel Caves, Tanami Expansion 2 and the feasibility study at Red Chris

data.title: Royal Gold outlines 2026 production and cash flow growth targets after record $1B revenue year
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Royal Gold (RGLD) Q4 2025

Management View

  • William Heissenbuttel, President, CEO & Director, opened by describing 2025 as a “transformational year for Royal Gold,” highlighting record revenue of $1 billion, operating cash flow of $705 million, and earnings of $466 million. He indicated, “After adjusting for unusual items throughout the year, net income was a record $510 million a 47% increase over 2024.”
  • The CEO emphasized the company’s gold focus, with gold contributing 78% of total revenue, and an adjusted EBITDA margin of 82%. He noted, “We acquired Sandstorm Gold and Horizon Copper, which allowed us to meaningfully grow and diversify our portfolio. We now have the largest and most diversified portfolio of mining assets in our sector.”
  • Heissenbuttel detailed several “material acquisitions” including Sandstorm Gold, Horizon Copper, Kansanshi gold stream, and interests in Warintza and Lawyers-Ranch projects. He also cited the “life of mine extension at Mount Milligan, the recently approved expansion at Khoemacau, and significant exploration success of Fourmile.”
  • Paul Libner, Senior VP & CFO, stated, “Revenue for the quarter was up strongly by 85% to $375 million. As Martin noted, during the quarter, we saw a combined new revenue of about $82 million from the Kansanshi Gold stream and the Sandstorm Horizon interest.”

Outlook

  • Management indicated that first quarter 2026 GEO sales are expected to be in line with Q4 2025 but “will be the lowest of the year and not reflective of the full year.” Full 2026 revenue guidance will be provided at the March 31 Investor Day.
  • The company expects its blended royalty rate at Cortez for 2026 to average 3.5% to 4% over production, compared to 2.6% in 2025.
  • Silver production attributable to Royal Gold at Khoemacau is projected at 1.45 million to 1.55 million ounces in 2026.
  • Debt repayment is now expected to be completed in early 2027, earlier than previously forecast.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for the quarter reached $375 million, with $32 million from Kansanshi and $49 million from Sandstorm and Horizon. Royalty revenue was $111 million, up 42% year-over-year.
  • Stream segment revenue totaled $265 million, over 110% higher than the same period last year. Operating cash flow was $242 million, a company record.
  • G&A expense was $17.6 million, elevated by nearly $4.5 million in integration costs for Sandstorm and Horizon, which are not expected to recur.
  • DD&A expense was $80 million, primarily due to new acquisitions.
  • Net income for the quarter was $94 million or $1.16 per share; adjusted net income was $155 million or $1.92 per share after excluding one-time items such as the $48 million loss on the Versamet Royalties share sale.
  • Outstanding debt decreased from $1.225 billion to $900 million by year-end, and further to $725 million in early 2026.

Q&A

  • Fahad Tariq, Jefferies: Asked about the deal pipeline and the outlook for copper projects. Daniel Breeze, SVP Corporate Development, responded that the pipeline “looks pretty strong at the moment,” with continued activity in both base and precious metals.
  • Cosmos Chiu, CIBC: Inquired about the Hod Maden project timeline, satisfaction with the technical report, and long-term strategy. Heissenbuttel confirmed satisfaction with the technical study and stated, “We would like to turn it into something that looks a little more familiar, where we don’t have the overrun risk, the operating cost risk. But that’s going to take some time.”
  • Derick Ma, TD Cowen: Asked about incentives for the Pueblo Viejo silver stream and why preliminary royalty revenue guidance isn’t provided. Heissenbuttel clarified the economic alignment and Libner explained that information rights limit preliminary estimates.
  • Tanya Jakusconek, Scotiabank: Sought clarification on Q1 GEO sales guidance and concerns about Pueblo Viejo silver recovery. Heissenbuttel and Raffield explained current recovery efforts and pointed to the upcoming technical report for further details.
  • Joshua Wolfson, RBC: Questioned Q1 production guidance and inventory levels. Martin Raffield stated that “lower estimate for Q1 down to delivery timing in general.”
  • Brian MacArthur, Raymond James: Queried capital commitments for Hod Maden. Heissenbuttel said the amount will depend on the construction decision timing, but restructuring before significant capital is preferred.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on clarity around new acquisitions, future deal pipeline, and asset development, displaying a neutral to slightly positive tone as they sought specifics on integration and future growth.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone, especially in emphasizing that one-time acquisition costs are now behind the company. Heissenbuttel stated, “Our underlying portfolio is performing well and after a record year in 2025, we're starting 2026 from a position of strength.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management showed increased focus on integration progress and clarity on future guidance, with management displaying more confidence about the company’s direction and financial strength.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from caution around acquisition-related “noise” in Q3 to emphasis on normalization and forward-looking stability in Q4.
  • Management’s tone moved from transitional to confident, with more explicit discussion of returning to “a steadier state” and achieving record results post-acquisitions.
  • Analysts’ questions transitioned from integration and transaction mechanics to operational outlook and capital allocation, particularly for Hod Maden and Pueblo Viejo.
  • Key metrics, such as revenue, operating cash flow, and adjusted net income, all reached new highs in Q4, with notable debt reduction following major acquisitions.
  • Strategic priorities evolved to focus on portfolio diversification, debt reduction, and daylighting value from recent acquisitions.

Risks and Concerns

  • One-time acquisition and integration costs impacted Q4 financials but are not expected to recur.
  • Higher G&A and DD&A expenses were attributed to new assets, with management stating these costs should subside.
  • The $48 million loss on the Versamet Royalties share sale was a notable one-time item.
  • Uncertainty remains around the Hod Maden restructuring and timing of capital commitments.
  • Metal recovery and delivery timing for assets like Pueblo Viejo present ongoing operational risks, as highlighted by analysts.

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized that the transformational acquisitions and portfolio expansions in 2025 have positioned Royal Gold for continued growth in 2026, with one-time integration costs now largely behind the company. Royal Gold anticipates providing more detailed guidance at its upcoming Investor Day, expects to keep delivering strong cash flow and revenue, and remains focused on extracting further value from its diversified, gold-focused asset base.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
